mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises rapidly, or signal loses predictive power with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to predict positive quarterly returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic momentum and triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases during easing periods, or rolling IC remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with stabilizing unemployment indicate accommodative monetary policy supporting a fragile labor market, which reduces recession risk and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low rates and stable unemployment fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with mean forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year drop in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and negative wealth effects, which reduces consumer spending and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns, with out-of-sample predictive accuracy below 50% or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside restrictive policy rates trigger inflation fears and expectations of economic slowdown, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation and restrictive rates are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in regression tests.
